purpose: A vertical flexbox layout primitive. Arranges children in a vertical column with configurable justification, alignment, and spacing. The vertical-only sibling of HBox. Rendered by the plugin as a pass-through frame using Figma Auto Layout (VERTICAL).
whenToUse: Stack child components vertically inside a page or panel
As a lightweight column when a full Panel is overkill
whenNotToUse: For a page layout with header/content/footer — use Page or DynamicPage
For a table of stacked items — use List
For a form field column — use Form / FormContainer
When items should reflow horizontally on wide viewports — use FlexBox
doRules: Use justifyContent to align children vertically (Start, Center, End, SpaceBetween)
Set height='100%' to fill parent when appropriate
Group semantically-related items in the same VBox
dontRules: Do not use VBox for form-field columns — use FormContainer
Do not nest deeply — becomes hard to reason about
Do not put a page shell inside VBox — use Page
Do not mix with absolute-positioned children

layoutGuidance.placement: Inside pages, panels, cards, custom composites
layoutGuidance.sizing: Height hugs content or fills parent; width hugs widest child

contentGuidance.contentRules: Children are any renderable components; not semantic

responsiveBehavior.S: Column stack (no horizontal reflow — for that use FlexBox)

requirements: role='group' when children share purpose; otherwise none
Focus order follows top-to-bottom visual order
Children provide their own accessible names
